machine_name: Bauer 1/3 HP 9 in. Benchtop Band Saw (SKU 70630)
who_its_for: This saw is for someone who needs an occasional-use band saw for light woodworking, scroll work, or hobby projects and wants the lowest possible entry price. The 9-inch throat and 3.5-inch max cut height make it suitable for thin stock, small curves, and craft work. It is not a resawing tool and is not suited to anyone expecting to cut hardwood regularly or work with stock thicker than 2-3 inches. If you're buying your first band saw and aren't sure you'll use it heavily, this is a low-risk entry point. If you already know you'll use it regularly, spend more and start with a 14-inch floor model.
owner_praise: Build quality above expectations for the price -- all-metal covers vs plastic-bodied competitors | Quick setup; table and blade arrive reasonably square out of the box | Compact footprint works well for small shops | Rack and pinion table tilt adjustment uncommon at this price point
owner_limitations: Motor struggles with curves in thicker hardwood | Included blade is functional but most owners replace it early | Fence requires initial drift adjustment | Resaw capability minimal -- suited to stock under 2 inches
upgrade_compatibility: Takes 59.5-inch blades 1/8 to 1/2 inch wide. Olson and Timber Wolf recommended aftermarket brands. 2.5-inch dust port compatible with standard shop vac hoses with adapter. No known aftermarket fence upgrade for this model.
compare_1_note: Near-identical specs at a slightly lower price; longer track record of owner reviews; safer choice if documented community data matters to you
compare_2_note: The Rikon 10-3061 is a 10-inch cast-iron deluxe benchtop with a 1/2 HP two-speed motor — significantly more cutting capability and community support for a modest premium.
notes: Thin community data -- SKU 70630 has zero reviews on HF site as of June 2026. Recommend holding in Draft until owner reviews accumulate. Specs verified against Harbor Freight product page.

machine_name: WEN BA3962 3.5-Amp 9-Inch Benchtop Band Saw
who_its_for: Buyers who need a capable benchtop bandsaw with stand for hobby cutting in softwood and light hardwood. Two-speed operation covers detail work and light resawing up to 6 inches deep. Not suited for resawing thick hardwood regularly or production-level work; step up to a 14-inch floor model for those needs.
owner_praise: Two-speed operation gives flexibility for different materials and blade widths | Worklight, fence, and miter gauge included at the price point | Stable table handles typical hobbyist cutting tasks | Three-in-one dust port fits 1-3/4, 2-3/4, and 4-inch hoses | 73.5-lb weight is manageable for benchtop repositioning
owner_limitations: Speed changes require loosening the motor mount to move the drive belt rather than a quick-release lever | Blade replacement is complex for first-time bandsaw owners | Worklight position is fixed and does not consistently illuminate the cut line | Resaw capacity limited to 6 inches restricts work with wider boards
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 72-inch blades 1/8 to 1/2 inch wide. Three-in-one dust port fits standard shop vac hoses. Fence and miter gauge included. Aftermarket upgrade path is limited; this is a self-contained tool rather than a platform with an accessory ecosystem.
compare_1_note: The Rikon 10-3061 offers a cast-iron table and 1/2 HP two-speed motor in a 10-inch benchtop, with more cut depth but no stand.
compare_2_note: The WEN BA3959 is a smaller 9-inch model at roughly half the price for the most basic cutting needs.
compare_3_note: The Jet JWBS-10OS steps up to 1/2 HP and a 4-inch dust port for buyers wanting more capability.
notes: Link verified 2026-07-06: WEN direct $399.25 (in stock). Not carried by Rockler/Woodcraft/Acme/Advanced Machinery. Amazon ASIN B09X1ZD8MH still active but price not machine-verifiable — add manually if desired. Single verified retailer.

machine_name: WEN BA3959 9-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: Entry-level buyers who need basic bandsaw capability for light hobby crafts, occasional curved cuts, or learning bandsaw fundamentals without significant investment. The 9-inch throat and 3.5-inch cut limit restrict this saw to softwoods, thin stock, and scrollwork. Buyers who plan to use a bandsaw more than occasionally or who want to work with any hardwood should start with a 10-inch model instead.
owner_praise: At 40 lbs and under $150, portability and price are unmatched in the category | Fence and miter gauge included at this price point | 2-1/2 inch dust port keeps the work area reasonably clean | Sufficient for light scrollwork and craft cutting in softwood
owner_limitations: 3.5-inch cut capacity limits the saw to thin stock only | Single-speed at 2500 FPM offers no flexibility for different materials | Community discussion of this specific model is limited | No horsepower rating published by manufacturer | Blade width maxes at 3/8 inch restricting any resawing
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 59-1/2 inch blades 1/8 to 3/8 inch wide. POWERTEC sells compatible 59-1/2 inch replacement blades. Standard 2-1/2 inch dust port. Fence and miter gauge included. No meaningful aftermarket accessory ecosystem for this model.
compare_1_note: The WEN BA3962 steps up to two-speed operation, a larger table, and an included stand for about $200 more.
compare_2_note: The Rikon 10-3061 offers a cast-iron 10-inch platform with a 1/2 HP two-speed motor for roughly double the price with significantly more cutting capability.
notes: Community data thin for this model. HP not published by manufacturer. Set to Draft pending more owner discussion.

machine_name: Jet JWBS-10OS 10-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: Buyers who want a brand-name 10-inch bandsaw with quality open stand, 1/2 HP motor, and Jet warranty coverage for regular hobby cutting. The 4-inch dust port supports proper dust management where smaller competitors use 2.5-inch ports. Buyers who expect to grow beyond 10-inch capacity within a year or two should save the investment and step directly to a 14-inch floor model.
owner_praise: Jet brand reputation and service network provides warranty assurance | 4-inch dust port is larger than most 10-inch competitors | Cast iron table tilts cleanly | LED worklight standard | 1/2 HP motor handles softwood and light hardwood reliably
owner_limitations: QA issues documented in community discussions particularly regarding wheel tires | Motor is underpowered for any meaningful hardwood resawing | 10-inch class limitation means buyers typically outgrow the saw within a couple of years | Table dimensions not published in standard manufacturer documentation
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 67-1/2 inch blades. Four-inch dust port connects to most shop vacuums directly. Jet sells replacement tires and blade guides. Takes standard 10-inch bandsaw blade sizes from Lenox, Timber Wolf, and others. Stock blade is 67.5 x .375 x .014 6TPI.
compare_1_note: The WEN BA3959 is a smaller 9-inch benchtop at a much lower price for basic cutting, without the Jet's cast-iron table or 4-inch dust port.
compare_2_note: The WEN BA3962 offers two-speed operation and a worklight at a lower price but with lighter-duty construction.
compare_3_note: The Rikon 10-3061 matches the 1/2 HP motor with two-speed capability and a 5-inch cut depth at a comparable price.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. Model now sold as successor JWB-10. Rockler $736.99 and Woodcraft $736.99 (new live URL) both in stock. Rockler price corrected from stale $629.99. Old dead Woodcraft link replaced.

machine_name: Rikon 10-3061 10-Inch Deluxe Band Saw
who_its_for: Buyers who want more from a 10-inch benchtop bandsaw than the basic 10-305 - specifically two-speed operation, a 1/2 HP motor, and a 5-inch cut depth. Good for woodturners and hobbyists cutting curved work in softwoods. Community QC reports suggest careful inspection at purchase; buyers who need production reliability or significant resawing capacity should move directly to a 14-inch floor model.
owner_praise: Two-speed operation (1515 and 3280 FPM) gives flexibility across blade types and materials | 1/2 HP motor is a meaningful step up from the 1/3 HP 10-305 | 5-inch cut depth is the maximum available in the 10-inch benchtop class | Rikon customer service responds quickly to warranty claims per community reports
owner_limitations: Quality control issues documented by owners including a wheel alignment plate breaking during early use | Miter slot is 5/8 inch non-standard limiting compatible aftermarket miter gauge options | Community feedback volume is lower than for the 10-305 | Weight not published by manufacturer
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 70-1/2 inch blades 1/8 to 1/2 inch wide at two speeds. Two-and-a-half-inch dust port. Note: 5/8-inch non-standard miter slot limits aftermarket miter gauge compatibility. Fence included. Rikon accessories compatible.
compare_1_note: The WEN BA3962 offers a similar two-speed 10-inch benchtop with a stand and worklight at a lower price, though with a lighter-duty frame.
compare_2_note: The Jet JWBS-10OS matches the 1/2 HP motor with a larger 4-inch dust port and open stand at a similar price.
notes: NOTE: This is a 10-inch bandsaw - user listed as 14-inch in error. Specs confirmed against Rikon product page. Weight not published. QC concerns documented in community. Set to Draft for QA review.

machine_name: Laguna 14|12 14-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: Buyers who want European bandsaw design features - ceramic blade guides, a precision aluminum fence, and a large 21.5x16 inch table - at a price below full industrial machines. The 1.75 HP motor on 110V handles demanding hobbyist resawing comfortably and the saw operates quietly. Tall woodworkers should note the lower-than-standard table height; buyers who need continuous production resawing should look at the Laguna 14|BX or a higher-HP competitor.
owner_praise: Ceramic blade guides eliminate heat buildup and extend blade life | No-tools blade installation and guide adjustment is faster than any competitor in this class | Runs quietly with exceptionally low vibration per consistent community reports | 21.5 x 16 inch table is among the largest in the 14-inch class | Premium Hi/Low aluminum fence is standard equipment
owner_limitations: Table height sits lower than standard 14-inch competitors - a problem for taller woodworkers | No miter gauge included; must be purchased separately | Laguna mobility kit reported as dangerously unstable in transport mode | Ceramic guides can spark during resawing with some owners reporting smoke from lower guides | 25-30 second coast-down time with no blade brake
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 115-inch blades 1/8 to 3/4 inch wide. Four-inch dust port. Laguna Ceramic Cool Blocks with replacements from Laguna and third parties. Compatible blades include Lenox and Woodslicer. No miter gauge included; standard 3/4-inch miter slot accepts aftermarket options. Hi/Low aluminum fence adjusts for drift.
compare_1_note: The Jet JWBS-14SFX offers a 1.75 HP steel-frame saw with 13-inch resaw at a lower price, though without the Laguna's ceramic guides and tool-free adjustments.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0555 offers a similar 14-inch cast-iron floor format at roughly half the price but with only 6 inches of native resaw capacity.
compare_3_note: The Powermatic PM1500B steps up to 3 HP and 14 inches of resaw capacity at a significantly higher price for industrial-level demands.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. Rockler $1,699 and Acme Tools $1,699 both in stock. Woodcraft still dead — replaced with Acme.

machine_name: Powermatic PM1500B 15-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: Buyers who need sustained high-capacity resawing that 1 and 1.75 HP machines cannot provide. The 3 HP motor at 230V handles full-width resawing in dense hardwood without strain, and the 14-inch resaw capacity covers the most demanding hobbyist and semi-professional work. Requires a dedicated 230V circuit and substantial floor space. Buyers who do not regularly push resawing limits should evaluate whether this investment is warranted compared to a 1.75 HP machine at roughly half the cost.
owner_praise: Three HP motor handles demanding hardwood resawing without strain - community consistently notes it cuts anything like butter | Excellent fit and finish with precision-ground cast iron surfaces | All adjustments are tool-free including tracking, guides, and tension | Dust collection performance is notably better than expected for a bandsaw | Welded steel frame at nearly 400 lbs dampens vibration to a level other saws cannot match
owner_limitations: Requires 230V dedicated circuit restricting placement and adding electrical cost | At nearly 400 lbs positioning requires a hand truck and a second person | Dust collection screen clogs with green wood shavings and thin strips during extended resawing | Price is significantly above 1.75 HP competitors that cover most hobbyist resawing needs | 80-inch overall height can be a problem for low-clearance shop spaces
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 153-inch blades 1/8 to 1 inch wide. Four-inch dust port. All adjustments tool-free. Includes rip fence and miter gauge. Large 21.5x16 cast iron table. Powermatic stocks OEM replacement blades (PM1500-021). Compatible with Woodslicer, Lenox, and Timber Wolf blades. 230V single-phase prewired. Mobile base sold separately.
compare_1_note: The Laguna 14|12 offers 1.75 HP with ceramic guides on 110V at roughly half the price for buyers who do not need 3 HP performance.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 offers a larger 17-inch throat with 2 HP at under half the price, trading the Powermatic's 3 HP and premium build for more capacity per dollar.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. Rockler, Woodcraft (now live), and Acme Tools all $4,179 in stock. Woodcraft link restored.

machine_name: Grizzly G0555 14-Inch 1 HP Bandsaw
who_its_for: The G0555 handles everyday cuts, curves, and light resawing up to 6 inches across a precision-ground cast iron table that outclasses aluminum alternatives at this price. The buyer wants an established 14-inch saw with a deep parts ecosystem and 20-plus years of community knowledge behind it. Buyers planning regular wide-board resawing should budget for the H3051 riser block or consider the G0555LX, which offers more capacity at a modest premium.
owner_praise: Precision-ground cast iron table consistently praised for flatness and stability — owners compare it favorably to far more expensive saws|Decades of continuous production means parts, riser blocks, replacement guides, and aftermarket fences are widely available|Fence and miter gauge included at no extra cost — comparable to what competitors charge as add-ons|H3051 riser block kit expands resaw capacity to 12 inches without replacing the saw|Two-speed pulley (1500/3200 FPM) allows basic material versatility across wood and some non-ferrous use
owner_limitations: 6-inch resaw capacity is the defining limitation — most buyers wanting serious resawing need the riser block kit|14 x 14 inch table is smaller than competing 14-inch saws at similar prices|1 HP motor shows strain on wide, dense hardwood resaw passes near maximum depth|Owners who outgrow it describe it as a stepping stone rather than a long-term keeper without the riser upgrade
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 92-1/2 to 93-1/2 inch blades, 1/8 to 3/4 inch wide; Grizzly H3051 riser block raises resaw capacity to 12 inches; Carter Products and Kreg aftermarket fences fit; Timber Wolf, Lenox, and Olson blades are direct replacements; 4-inch dust port connects to standard shop vacuum; 2-speed pulley belt is user-swappable
compare_1_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 steps up to a 17-inch throat and 2 HP for buyers who want significantly more capacity, at roughly double the price.
compare_2_note: Delta 28-400 has a larger table and steel frame at a lower original price, though the G0555 has a deeper parts and riser block upgrade ecosystem.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06: Grizzly direct $825 (in stock, +freight). Amazon B0000DD0AC ($935.29) not machine-verifiable — add manually if desired. Single verified retailer.

machine_name: Jet JWBS-14SFX 14-Inch Steel Frame Band Saw
who_its_for: The JWBS-14SFX handles demanding resawing, tight curves, and production-volume cuts with a 1.75 HP motor and 360 square inches of cast iron work surface. The buyer uses a bandsaw near-daily, values independently adjustable ball bearing guides over entry-level systems, and wants 13 inches of resaw capacity on a solid platform. Buyers needing two-speed operation for non-ferrous or detail work should compare the Rikon 10-326 at a similar price.
owner_praise: 360 square inch cast iron table (21 x 17 inches) provides stable support for large panels that smaller competing tables cannot match|Independently adjustable ball bearing blade guides above and below the table outperform the guide standard at lower price points|1.75 HP motor sustains hardwood resawing without the hesitation reported on 1 HP alternatives|Dual-height aluminum fence serves both resaw and rip work — owners report meaningful versatility without an aftermarket fence upgrade|Dual 4-inch dust ports substantially improve dust collection over single-port saws
owner_limitations: Single speed (3000 SFPM) limits use on non-ferrous materials or detail work requiring a slower blade speed|Stock blade requires replacement before serious use — owners consistently replace it immediately|Plastic hand wheels on tensioning and guide height adjustment are a cost-cutting decision owners notice at this price|Steel frame body is lighter and less vibration-dampening than fully cast construction at this price point
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 116-inch blades, 1/8 to 3/4 inch wide; dual 4-inch dust ports accommodate most shop dust collection systems; Kreg bandsaw fence fits as an aftermarket upgrade; Cool Blocks or Carter blade guides compatible; 1/2-inch blade included at purchase; 5-year JET warranty
compare_1_note: The Jet JWBS-14CS is Jet's budget 14-inch saw with a 1 HP motor and 6-inch resaw at a lower price for lighter-duty work.
compare_2_note: Rikon 10-326 offers two blade speeds and spring-loaded tool-free guides at a similar price — better suited to multi-material cutting.
compare_3_note: Laguna 14BX adds a magnetic disc brake and ceramic guide system at a higher price.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. Rockler $1,549 in stock. Acme Tools $1,549 (source flags end-of-life — monitor). Woodcraft still dead — replaced with Acme.

machine_name: Laguna 14|BX 14-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: The 14BX is the upmarket version of the 14|12, adding a one-piece heavier-gauge frame, cast iron wheels, a magnetic disc brake, and a magnetic on/off switch. The buyer is a serious woodworker who uses the bandsaw as a primary machine and values the disc brake as a meaningful safety and workflow upgrade over competing saws at this price. Buyers who can accept the 14|12's lighter frame and no brake will find the same 13-inch resaw capacity at a significantly lower price.
owner_praise: Disc brake that stops the blade in seconds is consistently cited by owners as more useful than expected — meaningfully changes shop workflow|One-piece heavy-gauge frame and cast iron wheels reduce vibration compared to the lighter 14|12 construction|Laguna ceramic guides outperform bearing guides in sustained resawing — owners report closer positioning and better cut quality|Dual light mounting positions and magnetic on/off switch are quality-of-life details absent from competing saws|Quick-release tension lever and worm gear rack and pinion guide adjustment streamline setup between blade changes
owner_limitations: Quality control issues documented by owners — upper wheel spoke alignment, guide blocks not flat or square, and wheel runout requiring factory correction|Sold exclusively through dealer network — no direct purchase option, limiting price comparison shopping|Blade not included at purchase — buyers must add 115-inch blade cost to initial purchase price|At $2,149 the price is above the Semi-Pro ceiling but the community frames it as a serious hobbyist upgrade rather than a production shop machine
upgrade_compatibility: Takes 115-inch blades, 1/8 to 3/4 inch wide with stock ceramic guides; Laguna Mini Guides accessory enables blades narrower than 1/4 inch; Laguna Resaw King carbide-tipped blade (3/4 x 115 inch) is the recommended resawing blade; optional mobility wheel kit available; dual 4-inch dust ports; 110V outlet on rear for optional work light (sold separately)
compare_1_note: Laguna 14|12 uses the same resaw capacity at a substantially lower price — the 14BX's frame, brake, and cast iron wheels justify the premium only for heavy daily use.
compare_2_note: Jet JWBS-14SFX costs less and adds a 360 sq in cast iron table, but lacks the disc brake and Laguna ceramic guide system.
compare_3_note: Rikon 10-326 offers two blade speeds and a comparable cast iron table at a significantly lower price.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. Rockler $2,149 and Advanced Machinery $2,149 (price now populated), 110V model. Woodcraft dead — replaced.

machine_name: Powermatic PM1800B 18-Inch Band Saw
who_its_for: The PM1800B handles industrial-volume resawing and continuous production work with 5 HP and 18 inches of cutting capacity in both height and width. The buyer is a professional woodworker, production shop operator, or institution running the saw near-continuously and expecting decades of service. Buyers who want 18-inch capacity for occasional resawing should weigh whether the PM1500B meets the same need at lower cost.
owner_praise: Solid cast iron upper and lower wheels create exceptional momentum for sustained heavy resaw — owners report smooth cutting through wide hardwoods at full 18-inch capacity|5 HP motor provides far more power than any cutting scenario demands — the saw never labors regardless of species or stock thickness|Two-speed pulley (2300/4400 SFPM) enables genuine versatility across wood species and demanding applications|Heavy-duty fence handles production-volume ripping without flex or drift|Overall build quality described as industrial-grade with minimal adjustment required out of the crate
owner_limitations: Requires dedicated 230V circuit — not suitable for shops without appropriate electrical infrastructure|616 lbs net weight makes repositioning impractical — owners recommend treating final placement as permanent|1-1/4 inch clearance at blade guides is tight for inserting and removing the maximum 1-1/2 inch blade — owners note the tolerance as a minor annoyance|Geared wheel for table tilt adjustment reported as sloppy compared to the rest of the machine's precision|No included work light at this price point — owners consider it an omission
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 160-inch blades up to 1-1/2 inches wide; solid steel resaw post included; high-end blade guides standard; blade tension release with electrical interlock; 230V dedicated circuit required; 3-phase version (PM1800B-3) available for production shop environments; Powermatic full parts and service network
compare_1_note: Powermatic PM1500B offers 15-inch capacity at a lower price — the practical choice for buyers who don't need full 18-inch production capacity.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 provides 17-inch capacity and 2 HP at a significantly lower price for buyers who don't need 5 HP output.
notes: Price not confirmed — typical range $3,499-$4,299; verify at Woodcraft and Advanced Machinery before going Live. Dust port diameter not published in available sources; left blank. Resaw capacity height and width both set to 18 per 18-inch bandsaw wheel size.

machine_name: Rikon 10-326 14-Inch Deluxe Band Saw
who_its_for: The Rikon 10-326 resaws up to 13 inches, runs at two blade speeds, and adjusts guides tool-free — a package designed for woodworkers who want a capable 14-inch saw without moving to premium Laguna or Powermatic pricing. The buyer uses a bandsaw regularly, values two-speed versatility for both wood and non-ferrous work, and wants a heavy cast iron table without paying for the disc brake and ceramic guide systems of the Laguna 14BX. Buyers who primarily cut wood at a single speed can save significantly with the Jet JWBS-14SFX.
owner_praise: Two blade speeds (1445 and 2950 FPM) give genuine multi-material capability owners consistently use for both wood and non-ferrous work|Spring-loaded tool-free blade guide adjustment is notably faster than competitor systems — owners cite this as a daily quality-of-life improvement|1.75 HP handles sustained hardwood resawing through wide boards including hard maple without hesitation|Quick-lock trunnion table angle system and quick-release blade tension lever streamline blade changes|5-year Rikon warranty with customer service cited as above-average for this price point
owner_limitations: Factory setup requires adjustment out of the box — owners consistently report needing to square the table and check guide alignment before first use|Frame weld alignment issues in documented cases — off-square installation requires hands-on correction before trusting the saw|Adjustment bolt on the fence head falls into the miter slot, making fence positioning a two-handed operation|Max blade width is 5/8 inch — narrower than the 3/4 inch standard on competing saws at this price
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 111-inch blades, 3/16 to 5/8 inch wide; 4-inch dust port in lower wheel compartment; 6-inch tall rip fence with re-saw bar included; spring-loaded guides accept standard Cool Blocks as replacements; Timber Wolf and Lenox 111-inch blades are direct fits; optional mobility kit available from Rikon
compare_1_note: Jet JWBS-14SFX has a larger table and single speed at a lower price — the better choice for buyers who don't need two-speed operation.
compare_2_note: Laguna 14BX adds a disc brake and ceramic guides but costs $550 more — justified only for heavy daily use where those features pay off.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 steps up to a 17-inch throat and 2 HP for buyers who need more capacity, at a higher price.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06. HIGHLAND LINK REMOVED (discontinued affiliate). Replaced with Rockler $1,899.99 and Woodcraft $1,359.99 (sale, new live URL), both in stock.

machine_name: Delta 28-400 14-Inch 1 HP Steel Frame Band Saw
who_its_for: The Delta 28-400 brings a cast iron table and two-speed steel frame construction to the 14-inch hobbyist market at a lower price than cast iron alternatives. The buyer wants proven Delta construction without the premium of a cast iron body — a meaningful step up from cast aluminum in rigidity at a competitive price. Buyers who plan to resaw regularly will hit the 6-inch limit quickly and should look at the Grizzly G0555 or G0555LX for an expandable alternative.
owner_praise: Cast iron table described by owners as flat and smooth — one reviewer compared surface quality favorably to a UniSaw|Steel frame provides better rigidity and vibration dampening than cast aluminum competition at this price point|Two-speed operation (1620/3340 FPM) gives practical versatility across wood types and some non-ferrous materials|Setup goes smoothly per owners who followed the manual — vibration-free operation reported after break-in|Delta brand carries decades of service infrastructure owners rely on for long-term parts and support
owner_limitations: 6-inch resaw capacity is the primary limitation — no riser block option exists for this model, making the capacity permanent|Steel frame rather than cast iron body means less mass and vibration dampening than comparable cast iron alternatives|Availability concerns — limited new stock at retail with buyers often competing with used market pricing|Table (15.75 x 18.875 inches) is adequate but smaller than the G0555LX and Rikon 10-326 at comparable prices
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 93-1/2 inch blades, 1/8 to 3/4 inch wide; two-speed standard; 115/230V dual voltage; no riser block upgrade path — 6-inch resaw is a permanent ceiling on this model; standard 14-inch aftermarket blades from Timber Wolf and Lenox fit directly; Delta parts and service network available
compare_1_note: Grizzly G0555 has the same cast iron table with a deeper upgrade ecosystem and riser block compatibility at a higher price.
compare_2_note: The Jet JWBS-14SFX steps up to 1.75 HP and 13-inch resaw for buyers who need more than the Delta's fixed 6-inch resaw limit, at a higher price.
notes: Verified 2026-07-06: Acme Tools $1,099.99 valid product page, backordered (1-2 month lead, orderable). Amazon link dead — removed. Single retailer found.

machine_name: Grizzly G0513X2 17-Inch 2 HP Bandsaw
who_its_for: Woodworkers who have outgrown 14-inch saws and want production-grade 17-inch throat and 12-inch resaw capacity with a 2 HP motor, in a currently-produced model with full Grizzly parts support. Requires a dedicated 220V circuit and does not ship with a plug. Buyers who do not need beyond 14-inch capacity should stay with a G0555-class saw; those needing 5 HP continuous production should step up to an 18-inch machine.
owner_praise: Resaws wide dense hardwood (9-inch white oak, thin veneer) without motor bog-down | High fit and finish with precise, positive controls | Rack-and-pinion roller blade guides stay accurate through full travel | Strong quality-for-price versus competing 17-inch saws
owner_limitations: Blade-tension and fence handle placement is awkward — you reach under the stock to adjust | Blade shroud makes guide and upper-bearing access difficult to set with a feeler gauge | No interlock on the tension lever, so the blade can pop off if started with tension released | Stock blade is poor quality and wide 1-inch blades are hard to install and remove
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 131-1/2 inch blades 1/8 to 1 inch wide at 1700/3500 FPM. Two 4-inch dust ports accept standard hose. Cast-iron fence and miter gauge included; Grizzly sells model-specific replacement and resaw blades. A foot-brake variant (G0513X2BF) is available. Prewired 220V; no plug included.
compare_1_note: The Powermatic PM1500B offers 15-inch capacity with 3 HP and premium cast-iron build at more than double the price.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0555 is a 14-inch 1 HP saw at under half the price for lighter resawing needs.
compare_3_note: The Laguna 14BX is a 14-inch saw with a disc brake and ceramic guides at a higher price for buyers who prioritize those features over throat capacity.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026 as the current-production successor to the discontinued G0513ANV anniversary edition. grizzly.com $1,850 verified in stock. Net weight not published (shipping weight 421 lbs). Requires 220V dedicated circuit. Verify community/owner data before moving to Live.

machine_name: Jet JWBS-14CS 14-Inch Closed Stand Bandsaw
who_its_for: Home-shop woodworkers who want a dependable, upgradeable traditional cast-iron 14-inch saw with Jet 5-year warranty and a closed storage stand at a moderate price. It handles general cutting and light resawing to 6 inches; add the riser block for taller stock. Buyers who resaw wide hardwood regularly should step up to the 1.75 HP JWBS-14SFX.
owner_praise: Proven traditional 14-inch design with long reported service life (10-plus years) | Easy near-assembled setup with single-knob tracking adjustment | 15 x 15 inch offset cast-iron table gives generous support in front of the blade | Clear Jet upgrade path — riser block, resaw fence, bearing guides, mobile base
owner_limitations: 1 HP is modest for heavy resawing and benefits from a riser block for taller stock | No fence included from the factory (separate accessory) | Polymer/nylon-bushing blade guides and the tensioner are common first upgrade targets | Dust collection under the lower wheel is mediocre and some units need wheel-balance tuning
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 93-1/2 inch blades 1/8 inch and wider at 3000 SFPM. 4-inch dust port. Jet OEM accessories: Riser Block Kit 708717 (extends resaw to ~12 inches), Deluxe Resaw Fence JRF-14N 714101, Carter-style bearing guides 708127, JMB-UMB mobile base 708118. Standard 14-inch aftermarket blades from Timber Wolf and Lenox fit.
compare_1_note: The JWBS-14SFX steps up to a 1.75 HP steel frame, 13-inch resaw, and larger table for more demanding work at a higher price.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0555 is a comparable 1 HP 14-inch cast-iron saw at a lower price with a deep riser-block and parts ecosystem.
compare_3_note: The Delta 28-400 is a two-speed 1 HP steel-frame 14-inch saw in the same class, though currently backordered.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026 as the budget/traditional Jet successor to the discontinued JWBS-14DXPRO. Rockler $1,154 verified in stock; Acme also carries it (price hidden until cart). Net weight 185 lbs per Jet spec. Fence not included from factory. Verify community data before Live.

machine_name: Grizzly G0948 10-Inch 1/2 HP Bandsaw
who_its_for: Hobbyists who want surprising resaw capacity — 6-1/8 inches, rivaling some 14-inch saws — in a compact benchtop footprint, with two-speed control and an LED work light. Good for lumber prep, veneer resawing, curves, and small-shop use. Buyers who need a standard miter slot for aftermarket jigs, or who plan heavy daily hardwood resawing, should step up to a 14-inch floor saw.
owner_praise: Exceptional 6-1/8 inch resaw capacity for a 10-inch saw — rivals some 14-inch machines | Clean, accurate, stable cuts with ball-bearing blade guides | Two blade speeds (1520/2620 FPM) plus an LED work light | Strong value and compact footprint for small shops
owner_limitations: Non-standard 8mm x 10mm miter slot blocks standard miter gauges and jigs | No quick-release blade tension lever | Cast-aluminum table and wheels feel less premium than cast iron | 1/2 HP universal motor is louder and less refined than an induction motor
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 71-1/2 to 72-1/2 inch blades 1/8 to 1/2 inch wide (Grizzly sells matched replacement and resaw blades). Stepped dust port accepts up to a 4-inch hose. Two-position resaw fence and miter gauge included. Bear Crawl mobile base (T31566) available. No riser block (fixed frame).
compare_1_note: The Jet JWBS-10OS is a 10-inch saw with a brand-name warranty and 4-inch dust port, but only about 4-1/8 inches of resaw versus the G0948 6-1/8.
compare_2_note: The Rikon 10-3061 is a 10-inch two-speed deluxe benchtop with a cast-iron table, but a smaller 5-inch resaw at a higher price.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0555 steps up to a full 14-inch floor saw with 1 HP and a cast-iron table for buyers ready to leave the benchtop class.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026. grizzly.com $380 on sale (list $425), in stock, 4.6/5 from 217 reviews — verified live via browser. Shipping weight 75 lbs (net not separately published). Non-standard 8mm x 10mm miter slot. Verify community data before Live.

machine_name: Grizzly G0555XH 14-Inch 1-3/4 HP Extreme Series Resaw Bandsaw
who_its_for: Serious hobbyists who want a full 12-inch resaw capacity in a 14-inch footprint without adding a riser block, powered by a 1-3/4 HP motor. The taller upper frame and cast-iron table suit frequent veneer and hardwood resawing. Buyers who only occasionally resaw, or who want the cheapest 14-inch entry, can save with the standard G0555; those needing a 17-inch-plus throat should look higher.
owner_praise: True 12-inch resaw capacity with no riser kit required | 1-3/4 HP handles hardwood resawing that bogs down 1 HP saws | Large 16 x 21-3/4 inch precision-ground cast-iron table | Quick-release tension, work light, and a closed cabinet with storage
owner_limitations: Guide bearings wear and need periodic replacement | Cast-iron frame design can flex when tensioning wide (over 1/2 inch) resaw blades | Needs careful tuning and a quality blade to reach full potential | At 334 lbs it is heavy and awkward to reposition
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 104 to 105 inch blades 1/4 to 3/4 inch wide at 1690/2950 FPM. 4-inch dust port. Two-position extruded-aluminum resaw fence and miter gauge included. Aftermarket guide bearings and carbide resaw blades fit; Grizzly Bear Crawl mobile base available.
compare_1_note: The standard Grizzly G0555 is a 1 HP 14-inch saw at under half the price, but tops out at 6 inches of resaw without a riser block.
compare_2_note: The Jet JWBS-14SFX offers a 1.75 HP steel frame and 13-inch resaw with a larger table at a comparable price, but a steel rather than cast-iron frame.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 steps up to a 17-inch throat and 2 HP for buyers who need more width, at a higher price.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026. grizzly.com $1,695 plus $259 freight, in stock, 4.8/5 from 57 reviews — verified live via browser. The 12-inch resaw with no riser is the standout. Shipping weight 334 lbs. Verify community data before Live.

machine_name: Rikon 10-324TG 14-Inch Open Stand Bandsaw
who_its_for: Woodworkers who want Rikon most popular 14-inch saw — tool-less blade guides, a quick-release tension lever, 13-inch resaw, and a 5-year warranty — on an open stand with a storage shelf. A strong daily driver for a serious home shop. Buyers who want a heavier cast-iron-wheel build or a disc brake should compare the deluxe 10-326 or a Laguna.
owner_praise: Tool-less blade guide adjustment is fast and well-liked | Quiet, smooth, accurate cuts with 13-inch resaw capacity | Two blade speeds (1445/2950 FPM) for wood and non-ferrous work | 5-year warranty and strong community support
owner_limitations: Fence can be fussy to set up | No miter gauge included | Cast-aluminum drive wheels are lighter than cast-iron-wheel competitors | Factory setup and guide alignment usually need fine-tuning
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 111-inch blades 3/16 to 3/4 inch wide at two speeds. 4-inch dust port. 6-inch tall rip fence with resaw bar included. Tool-less guides accept standard replacements; Rikon mobile base 13-326 with adapter plate 13-324 available. Shares 111-inch blades with the 10-326.
compare_1_note: The Rikon 10-326 deluxe adds a heavier build and larger handwheels at a higher price for buyers who want more mass.
compare_2_note: The Jet JWBS-14SFX offers a 1.75 HP steel frame and larger table at a similar-to-higher price, but with single-speed operation.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0555XH matches a 12-inch resaw with a 1.75 HP motor and cast-iron table at a higher price.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026. Woodcraft $1,199.99 (Model 10-324TG, item 167677), in stock/ships from manufacturer — verified live via browser. Net weight not published on retailer page (~218 lbs per secondary sources — confirm). Rikon most popular 14-inch. Verify weight and community data before Live.

machine_name: Grizzly G0514X2B 19-Inch 3 HP Extreme-Series Bandsaw w/ Motor Brake
who_its_for: Serious hobbyists and small-shop pros who want maximum wheel size and an 18-1/4 inch throat with a 3 HP motor and an electronic motor brake that stops the blade in about 3 seconds, at a value price. Requires a 220V circuit. Buyers who do not need 19-inch capacity can save with a 17-inch G0513X2; those wanting 16-inch resaw should look at an 18-inch premium saw.
owner_praise: Electronic motor brake stops the blade in about 3 seconds | Ample 3 HP power and stable, vibration-damped cuts | Excellent value — often 1,000 to 2,000 dollars less than comparable saws | Resaws fine veneer and thick hardwood with dual ball-bearing guides
owner_limitations: Some owners report vibration at higher speeds on individual units | At 512 lbs it is heavy and awkward to move | Only a 1-year warranty | Import-machine fit-and-finish can vary and benefit from tuning
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 143-inch blades 1/8 to 1-1/4 inch wide at 1700/3500 FPM. Dual 4-inch dust ports. Deluxe cast-iron fence with extruded-aluminum resaw fence and heavy-duty miter gauge included. Accepts aftermarket ceramic/Carter guides and carbide resaw blades; Bear Crawl mobile base available.
compare_1_note: The Grizzly G0513X2 is a 17-inch 2 HP saw at a lower price for buyers who do not need 19-inch capacity or a motor brake.
compare_2_note: The Powermatic PM1800B is an 18-inch 5 HP professional saw with 18-inch resaw at a much higher price and premium build.
compare_3_note: The Laguna 18BX is an 18-inch 3 HP saw with 16-inch resaw, ceramic guides, and a disc brake at a higher price.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026. grizzly.com $2,675, orderable (backordered), 4.7/5 from 44 reviews — verified live via browser. Requires 220V dedicated circuit. Shipping weight 512 lbs. Verify community data before Live.

machine_name: Laguna 18|BX 18-Inch 3 HP Bandsaw
who_its_for: Serious woodworkers who want a premium 18-inch saw with 16-inch resaw height, Laguna ceramic guides, a disc brake, and 1-1/4 inch blade capacity for large hardwood slabs and veneers. Buyers who resaw wide stock often and value ceramic guides and a fast-stopping brake. Those who need less capacity can save with the 14BX or 14|12; a Grizzly 19-inch offers more throat for less money.
owner_praise: 16-inch resaw and 1-1/4 inch blade capacity handle large hardwood slabs | Laguna ceramic guides run cool and hold position in sustained resawing | Disc brake stops large blades quickly | Arrives well packaged and tunes up to excellent cut quality
owner_limitations: Documented QC variance on some units (table flatness, trunnion ways) | Mixed reports on Laguna customer service | Shipping and accessory costs add up | At 410 lbs it needs a mobile base and is hard to reposition
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts 145-inch blades up to 1-1/4 inch wide. Dual 4-inch dust ports. Laguna Resaw King carbide blade and DXIII DriftMaster fence (both at Rockler) are common upgrades; ceramic guide inserts are replaceable; a mobile base is recommended.
compare_1_note: The Laguna 14BX is the 14-inch version with the same ceramic guides and disc brake at a much lower price and 13-inch resaw.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0514X2B is a 19-inch 3 HP saw with a wider throat and motor brake at a lower price, but with steel guides instead of ceramic.
compare_3_note: The Powermatic PM1800B is an 18-inch 5 HP saw with more power and 18-inch resaw at a higher price.
notes: New entry 7/6/2026. Rockler $3,299 (item 54757), in stock — verified live via browser. Also carried at Woodcraft and Advanced Machinery. Net weight ~410 lbs (460 shipping). Some documented QC variance. Verify additional specs/community data before Live.

machine_name: RYOBI 15 Amp 10 in. Table Saw with Folding Stand (RTS12)
who_its_for: This is a good first saw for someone setting up a garage shop on a tight budget or a homeowner who needs to rip a few boards and break down plywood now and then. It handles construction lumber and light furniture stock without complaint. If you plan to do fine joinery or want dead-accurate repeat cuts all day, you will outgrow it.
owner_praise: Cheap enough to buy without much thought | Light and easy to move around a one-car garage | Folding stand is quick to open and put away | Plenty of power for 2x material and plywood
owner_limitations: Fence needs checking and sometimes shimming for square rips | Miter gauge feels loose and cheap | 12 inch right rip limits wide sheet cuts | Dust pickup is weak even with a shop vac attached
upgrade_compatibility: Takes any standard 10-inch, 5/8-inch arbor blade, so a decent 40-tooth combo blade is the first worthwhile change. It does not accept a dado stack. The fence and stand are not really upgradeable, so plan to add an outfeed table or a simple shop-built crosscut sled if you want cleaner results.
compare_1_note: The RTS08 is a smaller 8-1/4 inch compact without a stand at a lower price.
compare_2_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS steps up to a rack-and-pinion fence and rolling stand for more accuracy.
compare_3_note: The Bosch 4100XC adds a gravity-rise stand and smoother cuts at a higher price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $229 VERIFIED on Home Depot page. Big-box retailer (not a preferred woodworking affiliate); Home Depot is monetizable via the site Impact tag. HP not published (15A). Community substantive.

machine_name: RYOBI 13 Amp 8-1/4 in. Compact Portable Jobsite Table Saw (RTS08)
who_its_for: This is about the most affordable way into a real table saw, so it fits a renter, a beginner, or anyone who needs an occasional saw that tucks away on a shelf. It does great with plywood, trim, and thin stock on a workbench. If you want a stand, deeper cuts, or a wide selection of blades, step up to a 10-inch saw.
owner_praise: One of the cheapest new saws that still cuts clean | Light enough to store on a shelf and carry one-handed | Rack and pinion fence slides smoothly and holds square | Huge owner base means plenty of help and reviews online
owner_limitations: No stand, so you supply your own bench | Smaller 8-1/4 inch blade limits cut depth and blade choices | Little table offers weak support for big sheets | Not happy ripping thick hardwood or running for long stretches
upgrade_compatibility: It uses 8-1/4 inch, 5/8-inch arbor blades, a smaller and less common size, so pick up a good thin-kerf blade for cleaner cuts. There is no dado capability. Since it ships without a stand, the first real add-on is a solid bench or a small rolling table, plus a shop vac on the 2.5-inch port.
compare_1_note: The RTS12 is a full 10-inch saw with a folding stand for a bit more money.
compare_2_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS is a far more capable jobsite saw with a rolling stand.
compare_3_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS offers 35 inches of rip and a fold-and-roll stand at a higher price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $149 VERIFIED on Home Depot page, 2266 ratings. 8-1/4 inch blade. Big-box retailer (Impact-monetizable). HP not published (13A).

machine_name: CRAFTSMAN 10 in. 15 Amp Portable Jobsite Table Saw with Folding Stand (CMXETAX69434502)
who_its_for: A solid pick for a weekend woodworker or a DIYer doing home projects who wants a familiar name and a real aluminum top without spending much. It manages framing lumber, trim, and plywood breakdown well. Anyone chasing furniture-grade accuracy or running a dado stack should keep shopping.
owner_praise: Very low price for a saw with a metal table and stand | Flat cast aluminum top holds a straight cut | Roll cage frame shrugs off jobsite bumps | Fence locks at both ends and stays where you set it
owner_limitations: Fence often needs squaring before you trust it | Folding the stand takes a couple of steps | 12 inch right rip is tight for big sheets | Guard assembly is awkward to work around
upgrade_compatibility: Runs standard 10-inch, 5/8-inch arbor blades, and swapping in a good 40-tooth blade sharpens up cut quality right away. There is no dado capability on this model. The fence and stand are what they are, so a shop-made crosscut sled and an outfeed surface are the practical upgrades.
compare_1_note: The Ryobi RTS12 is a similar sub-$250 jobsite saw with a folding stand.
compare_2_note: The Genesis GTS10SB is a cheaper full kit with stand and accessories.
compare_3_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS is a large step up in fence accuracy and rip capacity.
notes: NEEDS QA 7/6/2026. Price $199 REPORTED (Lowe's page is JS-gated; corroborated by Pro Tool Reviews / SlickDeals). Big-box only (Lowe's), no preferred affiliate link. Confirm live price before Live.

machine_name: Genesis GTS10SB 10 in. 15 Amp Table Saw with Stand
who_its_for: This suits a hobbyist or occasional DIYer who wants a complete package that works out of the box for light woodworking and small projects. It is happiest with thinner stock, plywood, and craft work. If you regularly rip thick hardwood or need to run the saw hard for hours, it will frustrate you.
owner_praise: Comes with stand, blade, and accessories for one low price | Self-aligning fence locks square without much fuss | Die-cast aluminum top is flatter than plastic-top rivals | Light and simple to set up and store
owner_limitations: Overload breaker can trip when ripping thick hardwood | Blade guard is flimsy and awkward | Not meant for heavy or all-day cutting | Parts and support are harder to find than major brands
upgrade_compatibility: It takes standard 10-inch, 5/8-inch arbor blades, so a better carbide blade is the obvious first move. There is no dado support. The 2-1/4 inch dust port is a little undersized, so you may want an adapter to fit a common shop vac hose, and a shop-built sled improves crosscut accuracy.
compare_1_note: The Ryobi RTS08 is a cheaper 8-1/4 inch compact from a bigger brand.
compare_2_note: The Craftsman CMXETAX has a sturdier roll cage and name-brand support.
compare_3_note: The Ryobi RTS12 steps up to a rack-and-pinion fence and folding stand.
notes: NEEDS QA 7/6/2026. Price $223 REPORTED (Walmart; Amazon listing was out of stock). Dust port 2-1/4 in (nonstandard). Some specs (45 depth, weight) not cleanly published. Confirm availability/price before Live.

machine_name: DeWalt DWE7491RS 10-Inch Jobsite Table Saw with Rolling Stand
who_its_for: This is the saw a lot of woodworkers land on when they want jobsite portability without giving up rip accuracy. It suits a garage shop where the saw has to fold away between projects, and it holds up fine for hobbyists cutting cabinet parts, furniture stock, and sheet goods.
owner_praise: The rack-and-pinion fence locks down square and stays put | The 15-amp motor rips hardwood without bogging | The rolling stand goes from folded to ready in a few seconds | Storage on the saw keeps the guard, wrenches, and push stick close by
owner_limitations: It is heavy and awkward to lift on and off the stand by yourself | The stock miter gauge feels cheap next to the rest of the saw | Above-table dust collection lets fine dust escape | The price has crept up over the years and now sits near the top of this range
upgrade_compatibility: It takes standard 10-inch blades and stacked dado sets up to 13/16 inch with the dado throat plate, sold separately. The fence and rails are part of the saw and are not meant to be swapped, so most owners upgrade the blade and add a shop vac at the 2.5-inch port.
compare_1_note: The Bosch 4100XC swaps the rolling stand for a gravity-rise design and adds soft-start.
compare_2_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS offers 35 inches of rip, the widest here.
compare_3_note: The Ryobi RTS12 is a much cheaper basic jobsite saw for lighter work.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $649 at Acme (cart-gated on page; consistent street price, at the top of the Hobbyist band). Sits above $650 at some retailers. 2 HP (15A).

machine_name: Bosch 4100XC-10 10-Inch Worksite Table Saw with Gravity-Rise Wheeled Stand
who_its_for: If you move the saw around the shop or take it to a site, the gravity-rise stand is the reason to pick this one. It fits a hobbyist who wants smooth, quiet cuts and a genuinely good safety setup, and it works well for furniture and trim work.
owner_praise: The gravity-rise stand tips up and rolls over gravel and thresholds with almost no effort | Soft-start keeps the motor quiet and easy on the arms | The SquareLock fence sets parallel and stays there | Cuts come out smooth with very little vibration
owner_limitations: The stand is heavy and takes up a lot of floor space when folded | The fence occasionally drifts and wants a quick recheck | Rip capacity tops out at 30 inches, less than some saws in this price range | There is not much onboard storage for accessories
upgrade_compatibility: It runs standard 10-inch blades and accepts a stacked dado set with the optional dado insert. The fence and rails are integral to the saw, so upgrades usually mean a better blade and a shop vac hooked to the 2.5-inch port. The gravity-rise stand is specific to Bosch and is included.
compare_1_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS has a bigger 32.5 inch rip and rolling stand at a similar price.
compare_2_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS gives the widest rip in the class at 35 inches.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0962 steps up to a stationary hybrid with cast iron and a 4-inch port.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $599 at Acme (cart-gated on page; consistent street price). HP not published (15A, marketed 4 max developed HP). Cut depths flagged for spec-sheet confirm.

machine_name: Metabo HPT C10RJS 10-Inch Jobsite Table Saw with Fold and Roll Stand
who_its_for: The big draw here is 35 inches of rip capacity, the widest in this group, which makes breaking down full sheets of plywood a lot easier. It fits a hobbyist who works with sheet goods often and wants a large table and a fold-and-roll stand at a fair price.
owner_praise: The 35-inch rip capacity handles wide sheet goods that cramp other jobsite saws | The work table is roomy and supports bigger panels | The motor has an electric brake and soft start that feel reassuring | For what it costs, you get a lot of saw
owner_limitations: The bevel adjustment is stiff and the lock can nudge the angle a few degrees off | The stock fence is fine but takes some fiddling to trust | It is a heavy saw to move even on the rolling stand | You see it less at woodworking stores, so hands-on comparison is harder
upgrade_compatibility: It takes standard 10-inch blades and accepts a stacked dado set with the dado insert. The fence and rails come with the saw and are not designed to be replaced, so most owners upgrade the blade first and connect a vacuum to the 2.5-inch dust port. The fold-and-roll stand is included.
compare_1_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS has a similar rolling stand but 32.5 inches of rip.
compare_2_note: The Bosch 4100XC trades rip width for a gravity-rise stand and soft-start.
compare_3_note: The SawStop CTS adds a flesh-sensing safety brake at a higher price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $549 VERIFIED in Acme page structured data (was $599). Sold as C10RJSM at Acme. HP not published (15A).

machine_name: Ridgid R4514 15-Amp 10-Inch Pro Jobsite Table Saw with Stand
who_its_for: This one appeals to hobbyists who like the idea of Ridgid lifetime service coverage and want a saw with a deep 3-1/2 inch cut for thick stock. It works for general shop projects and holds a spot as a solid Home Depot pickup, especially if you already buy Ridgid tools there.
owner_praise: The 3-1/2 inch cut depth handles thicker material than most jobsite saws | The 15-amp motor with soft-start ramps up smoothly | The Lifetime Service Agreement covers parts and service for free once you register | The cast-aluminum table feels solid for the price
owner_limitations: The clamping fence is the part owners complain about most and takes patience to set parallel | It uses a clamp fence instead of the rack-and-pinion setup rivals have | It is a heavy saw to move around | You can only buy it at Home Depot, so comparing it in person against other brands is tough
upgrade_compatibility: It runs standard 10-inch blades and accepts a stacked dado set with the dado throat plate. Aftermarket zero-clearance and dado inserts are available. The fence is the common upgrade target, though not a simple swap, so most owners tune the stock fence, fit a better blade, and run a vacuum on the 2.5-inch port.
compare_1_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS uses a rack-and-pinion fence instead of a clamp fence.
compare_2_note: The Bosch 4100XC adds a gravity-rise stand and soft-start.
compare_3_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS offers 35 inches of rip for less.
notes: NEEDS QA 7/6/2026. Price $549 REPORTED (Home Depot exclusive; no preferred affiliate link and no Amazon ASIN for the saw). HP not published (15A). Confirm live price; decide affiliate approach.

machine_name: Grizzly G0962 10" 2 HP Open-Stand Hybrid Table Saw
who_its_for: This is for the hobbyist who wants a stationary saw with cast-iron trunnions and a real 4-inch dust port but does not want to spend cabinet-saw money. It fits a dedicated garage or basement shop where the saw stays put most of the time. If you are stepping up from a portable saw and plan to do furniture and cabinet work, it gives you room to grow.
owner_praise: Cast-iron table and cabinet-mounted trunnions keep cuts accurate and calm the vibration | The 4-inch dust port and enclosed shroud pull chips well for a saw at this price | The 2 HP motor powers through thick hardwood without complaint | Comes prewired for a normal 120V outlet and rolls on a built-in mobile base
owner_limitations: The stock fence works but many owners swap it for something beefier down the road | Getting the fence and trunnions dialed in at setup takes some patience | The open stand lets a bit of dust drift out the bottom | It ships by freight, which adds cost and the rare dinged-in-transit story
upgrade_compatibility: Runs any 10-inch blade and accepts a stacked dado up to 13/16 inch. The fence and rails can be replaced with a Biesemeyer-style or T-glide setup if you want more precision, and rewiring to 240V is straightforward for a bit more headroom. Pair the 4-inch port with a real dust collector and it stays clean.
compare_1_note: The SawStop CTS adds a safety brake but drops to a portable design.
compare_2_note: The SawStop CNS steps up to a safety brake on a full contractor saw.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G1023RL is a full 3 HP cabinet saw for more power.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $1,195 VERIFIED on grizzly.com (plus ~$199 freight), In Stock. 2 HP induction. Shipping weight 297 lbs.

machine_name: SawStop Compact Table Saw (CTS-120A60)
who_its_for: This is for the woodworker who puts safety first and wants the flesh-sensing brake in the smallest, most affordable SawStop. It suits a tight shop or anyone who carries the saw between a garage and a job, since it weighs under 70 pounds but still swings a full 10-inch blade. Parents teaching kids, or anyone who has had a close call, gravitate to this one.
owner_praise: The safety brake stops the blade on skin contact and is the whole reason people choose it | The rack-and-pinion fence squares up easily and holds its setting | Light enough to carry by the handle yet cuts a full 3-1/8 inches deep | Build quality and finish feel a cut above most portable saws
owner_limitations: It will not run a dado stack, so grooves and rabbets take another method | The 2-1/2 inch dust port and collection are only so-so | The direct-drive motor is louder and buzzier than a belt-driven saw | You pay extra for the safety tech, and a brake trip means buying a new cartridge and usually a new blade
upgrade_compatibility: Takes standard 10-inch blades but no dado sets, since the compact drivetrain cannot handle them. A folding stand and zero-clearance inserts are available from SawStop, and a shop vac on the 2-1/2 inch port improves cleanup. If dado capability matters, SawStop points buyers to its larger Jobsite Saw Pro instead.
compare_1_note: The SawStop CNS is a full contractor saw with the same brake and more power.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0962 is a stationary hybrid with more power but no safety brake.
compare_3_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS is a cheaper jobsite saw without the brake.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $899 VERIFIED (sawstop.com and Rockler). HP intentionally unpublished by SawStop (15A). Compact/portable, not contractor/hybrid.

machine_name: Delta 36-725T2 10 in. Contractor Table Saw
who_its_for: A good first serious saw for someone moving out of the jobsite-saw world who wants a real induction motor and a fence they can trust. It suits a one-car-garage shop where you want cabinet-saw manners without cabinet-saw money or a 240V circuit. If most of your work is furniture and cabinet parts on a budget, this is a sensible landing spot.
owner_praise: Belt-driven induction motor runs quiet and rips hardwood without bogging | The T-square fence locks down square and stays put cut after cut | Cast-iron main table is flat and gives a smooth work surface | Hard to beat the capability you get for around seven hundred dollars
owner_limitations: Dust collection is the weak spot and really wants a shop vac helping the lower port | The extension wings are stamped steel, not cast iron | A few owners got saws banged up in shipping and found support slow to respond | It runs on 120V only, so there is no bump to 240V later
upgrade_compatibility: Takes any standard 10-inch blade and handles a 13/16-inch stacked dado set with the throat plate. The Biesemeyer-style fence and rails can be swapped for a longer aftermarket setup, and adding an over-arm dust hood plus a shop vac on the lower port cleans up the dust story. It bolts onto a common mobile base.
compare_1_note: The Grizzly G0962 is an enclosed hybrid with a 4-inch dust port and 2 HP.
compare_2_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS is a cheaper portable jobsite saw.
compare_3_note: The SawStop CTS trades power for a flesh-sensing safety brake.
notes: NEEDS QA 7/6/2026. Price ~$679 REPORTED (Lowe's reference; Amazon page bot-blocked). No preferred-retailer visible price; big-box/Amazon channel. 1.75 HP induction. Confirm price before Live.

machine_name: Skilsaw SPT99-11 10" Heavy-Duty Worm Drive Table Saw with Stand
who_its_for: This is the saw for someone who wants jobsite portability with more grunt than a typical direct-drive portable, thanks to worm-drive gearing borrowed from Skilsaw circular saws. It fits a hobbyist who moves the saw around or works out of a garage and occasionally hauls it to a site. Anyone ripping a lot of thick, dense stock on a portable will appreciate the extra torque.
owner_praise: The worm-drive motor keeps its speed through hardwood that bogs down lighter saws | You get a 30-1/2 inch rip and a deep 3-5/8 inch cut at 90 degrees | The rack-and-pinion fence sets square and stays there | The rolling stand with big wheels handles rough ground and loads into a truck easily
owner_limitations: The plastic dust chute is flimsy and tends to crack | The stamped miter gauge feels like an afterthought | The stand leveling feet spin around while you try to adjust them | The stand assembly instructions are vague and take some trial and error
upgrade_compatibility: Runs any 10-inch blade and takes a stacked dado set with the optional dado throat plate. The fence and rails are built into the saw and are not really meant to be swapped, so upgrades center on a better blade, a real miter gauge, and a shop vac on the 2-1/2 inch port. It ships on its own heavy-duty rolling stand.
compare_1_note: The DeWalt DWE7491RS is a lighter direct-drive jobsite saw with a rolling stand.
compare_2_note: The Bosch 4100XC adds a gravity-rise stand and soft-start.
compare_3_note: The Metabo HPT C10RJS gives the widest 35 inch rip in the jobsite class.
notes: NEEDS QA 7/6/2026. Price ~$649 REPORTED (Amazon; Acme masks price; Atlas lists $799.99). Worm-drive direct. HP not published (15A). 45 depth estimated. Confirm price before Live.

machine_name: SawStop Contractor Saw CNS175 (30" Premium Fence)
who_its_for: This is the saw for a home woodworker who wants the blade-stopping safety system without paying cabinet-saw money. It fits a two-car-garage shop and runs on a standard 120V outlet out of the box, so you can plug it in the day it arrives. If you have kids around the shop or you just value keeping all ten fingers, this is the entry point into the SawStop world.
owner_praise: The safety brake genuinely works and gives real peace of mind | Build quality feels a step above most contractor saws | The riving knife and guard are easy to take off and put back so people actually use them | Runs quiet and smooth on a belt drive
owner_limitations: The 1.75 HP motor slows down in thick, dense hardwood | You pay a premium mostly for the safety tech | Dust collection is only okay because the back of the cabinet is open | A brake activation means buying a new cartridge and a new blade
upgrade_compatibility: You can step up to a 36 or 52 inch T-Glide fence and rail at purchase or later, and the saw takes an 8-inch dado stack with the dedicated dado brake cartridge and insert. Convert the motor to 240V for a slightly stronger pull, add the matching mobile base, and bolt on the overarm dust collection guard for better above-table pickup.
compare_1_note: The SawStop CTS is the smaller portable saw with the same safety idea.
compare_2_note: The SawStop PCS steps up to a 3 HP cabinet saw with better dust collection.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G1023RL trades the safety brake for a full 3 HP cabinet saw at a similar price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $1,999 VERIFIED on sawstop.com Build & Price (CNS175, 30 in Premium Fence). Also at Acme/Rockler. 1.75 HP.

machine_name: Jet ProShop II 10" Table Saw (JPS-10, 30" rip)
who_its_for: Reach for the ProShop II if you want a Jet-quality saw that runs on a regular 115V circuit and you do not have 240V in the garage. It suits a hobbyist building furniture and small cabinets who wants good safety features and tidy onboard storage. The stamped-wing version keeps the price down, while the cast-wing option is there if you want a flatter, heavier top.
owner_praise: The magnetic paddle switch and quick-release riving knife make daily use simple | Onboard storage keeps the wrenches, guard, and push stick with the saw | The single-belt design is easy to tension and runs smooth | Dust pickup improved over the previous ProShop
owner_limitations: The 1.75 HP motor on 115V asks you to slow down in thick hardwood | The stamped steel wings on the base model can flex, so many people upgrade to cast iron | A 30 inch rip fence is a little tight for full sheets of plywood | It ships by freight and returns carry a restocking fee
upgrade_compatibility: You can buy the cast-iron-wing or 52 inch fence version instead of upgrading later, and the saw accepts an 8-inch dado stack up to 13/16 inch. Add a compatible mobile base, run a real 4-inch hose to the port, and fit a quality blade. This model is 115V only, so there is no 240V conversion.
compare_1_note: The Laguna Fusion F2 is a hybrid with standout dust collection at a similar price.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0962 is a cheaper hybrid with a 4-inch port.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G1023RL steps up to a 3 HP cabinet saw.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $2,089 VERIFIED on Woodcraft page (JPS-10, stamped-wing 30 in). 115V only. 1.75 HP.

machine_name: Grizzly G1023RL 10" 3 HP 240V Cabinet Table Saw
who_its_for: This is the saw for a woodworker who wants full 3 HP cabinet-saw muscle and does not want to spend north of three grand to get it. It needs a 240V circuit and a spot it can stay put, so it suits a dedicated garage or basement shop. If you rip a lot of thick hardwood and want a heavy, stable machine that will outlast lighter saws, this is a lot of iron for the price.
owner_praise: The 3 HP motor powers through thick hardwood without slowing | Heavy cast-iron trunnions and table stay dialed in once you set them | It runs quiet and smooth on the Poly-V belt | Widely called the best value in full-size cabinet saws
owner_limitations: The included Shop Fox Classic fence is the weak spot and many owners swap it | Freight shipping sometimes brings a warped insert or minor damage to sort out | At 471 pounds you will want a second person and a mobile base to move it | The warranty is only one year
upgrade_compatibility: Takes an 8-inch dado stack up to 13/16 inch with the included insert, and you can add long rails to push rip capacity to 53 inches. A Biesemeyer-style aftermarket fence is a popular upgrade over the stock Shop Fox, and a mobile base makes it rollable. It is already 240V, so no motor conversion is needed.
compare_1_note: The SawStop CNS trades some power for a flesh-sensing safety brake.
compare_2_note: The Powermatic PM2000B is a premium 3 HP cabinet saw with a heavier trunnion.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G0962 is a cheaper 2 HP hybrid for smaller shops.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $2,371 VERIFIED on grizzly.com (list $2,695, plus ~$349 freight), In Stock. 3 HP, 240V.

machine_name: Laguna Fusion F2 Table Saw (1.75 HP, 36" rip)
who_its_for: The F2 Fusion is aimed at the home shop that cares about a clean workspace and easy mobility. Its enclosed base and above-blade guard pickup catch far more dust than an open contractor saw, and the casters are already built into the cabinet so you can roll it out of the way. It runs on a 110V circuit and suits furniture and cabinet work in a small to medium shop.
owner_praise: Dust collection is genuinely good with pickup above and below the blade | The mobility kit is built into the cabinet so you can roll it anywhere | The High/Low Euro fence flips for thin stock and pulls back for safer crosscuts | The cast-iron top and arbor adjustments feel smooth and precise
owner_limitations: At 1.75 HP on 110V it is not built for heavy hardwood ripping | A 36 inch rip fence is a bit tight for full sheets | The fence and throat plate feel a step below the bigger F3 | Some owners wish it had more power for the price
upgrade_compatibility: The saw accepts an 8-inch dado stack up to 3/4 inch using the Laguna Fusion dado insert, and it comes with the High/Low fence and rail already. Add a zero-clearance insert, connect a 4-inch hose to the main port and a small hose to the blade-guard port for near-dustless cutting, and drop in a better blade. The built-in casters mean no separate mobile base, and it is 110V only.
compare_1_note: The Jet ProShop II is a similar 115V hybrid at a close price.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G0962 is a cheaper hybrid with a 4-inch port.
compare_3_note: The SawStop CNS adds a flesh-sensing safety brake.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $1,999 VERIFIED on Rockler page (F2 Fusion, 36 in fence). 110V only. 1.75 HP.

machine_name: SawStop Professional Cabinet Saw 3HP (PCS31230-TGP236, 36" T-Glide)
who_its_for: This is the saw for woodworkers who want the safety brake and are willing to pay for it. It fits serious hobbyists and pros running a 240V shop who want a cabinet saw that arrives well tuned and stays accurate. If finger safety is high on your list, this is the obvious pick.
owner_praise: The skin-sensing brake works as promised and gives real peace of mind | Fence and blade alignment are quick to set and hold their tune | The T-Glide fence slides smoothly and locks square every time | Three horsepower rips hardwood without bogging down
owner_limitations: The overarm dust collection could be better and many owners rig up their own fix | The blade guards block your view of the cut | Saws can arrive slightly out of alignment after shipping | You pay to replace a brake cartridge if it fires, including on wet or metallic stock
upgrade_compatibility: Takes any standard 10-inch blade and an 8-inch dado stack up to 29/32 inch with the separate dado brake cartridge and insert. Works with SawStop overarm dust collection, integrated router tables, outfeed tables, and a mobile base, and the 36 inch rails can be swapped for a 52 inch setup.
compare_1_note: The SawStop ICS is the heavier industrial version of the same safety saw.
compare_2_note: The Powermatic PM2000B is a 3 HP cabinet saw without the safety brake.
compare_3_note: The SawStop CNS is a lighter, cheaper contractor saw with the same brake.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $3,881 VERIFIED on sawstop.com (3 HP, 36 in T-Glide). 3 HP, 240V.

machine_name: Powermatic PM2000B 10" Table Saw 3HP, 30" Rip w/ Accu-Fence
who_its_for: This one is for the woodworker who wants a heavy, smooth-running cabinet saw with the Powermatic name and finish. It suits pros and dedicated hobbyists who value a rigid trunnion and buttery adjustments over the lowest price. The 30 inch rip fits a shop building furniture and cabinets that does not need full sheet-ripping width out of the box.
owner_praise: The one-piece cast-iron trunnion makes it rigid and almost vibration-free | Swapping the riving knife takes no tools, and a second low-profile knife comes in the box | The digital bevel readout and hydraulic caster base are handy upgrades on the B version | Three horsepower handles furniture-shop hardwood with room to spare
owner_limitations: You only get a standard throat plate, and the factory dado and zero-clearance inserts are pricey | A few owners have dealt with shipping damage or slow-to-find small plastic parts | The price is high for a 3 HP saw when cheaper cousins perform close to it | The digital readout and hydraulic base add cost that not everyone feels they need
upgrade_compatibility: Runs any 10-inch blade and a dado stack with the matching Powermatic dado insert. The 30 inch Accu-Fence rails can be extended to a 50 inch setup, and the saw pairs with an overarm blade guard, better dust collection through the 4-inch port, and the built-in hydraulic mobility kit.
compare_1_note: The Jet XactaSaw Deluxe is a 3 HP cabinet saw from the same parent company for less.
compare_2_note: The SawStop PCS adds a flesh-sensing brake at a similar price.
compare_3_note: The Grizzly G1023RL is a 3 HP cabinet saw at less than half the price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $5,092 VERIFIED on Rockler page (item 68568). 3 HP, 240V. 45 depth is the widely cited figure (powermatic.com page returned empty).

machine_name: Jet Deluxe XactaSaw 10" Table Saw 3HP, 30" Rip (JTAS-10XL30-DX)
who_its_for: The Deluxe XactaSaw fits the woodworker who wants a proven, well-finished cabinet saw and likes the Jet name and warranty. It works well for furniture and cabinet shops running 240V that want a saw arriving nearly tuned. Buyers who care about dealer support and a long track record tend to land here.
owner_praise: Owners say it shows up well aligned and cutting smoothly right away | The Xacta II fence is heavy and stays square once set | The shrouded blade and Poly-V belt pull dust down well | The modular guard and riving knife setup earns long-term loyalty, some past a decade
owner_limitations: The trunnion is a notch below the Powermatic built by the same parent company | Older units shipped without a riving knife | The tilt lock can strip if you crank it too hard at a bevel | Next to a Grizzly, some owners feel the build alone does not explain the price gap
upgrade_compatibility: Accepts any 10-inch blade and an 8-inch dado stack up to 13/16 inch with the Jet dado insert. The 30 inch Xacta rails can be moved up to a 50 inch set, and Jet sells a universal mobile base, low-profile riving knives, and matching guard parts. The 4-inch port takes a shop dust collector directly.
compare_1_note: The Powermatic PM2000B is the premium sister saw with a heavier trunnion.
compare_2_note: The Grizzly G1023RL is a 3 HP cabinet saw for far less money.
compare_3_note: The SawStop PCS adds a flesh-sensing safety brake.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $4,014 VERIFIED on Rockler page (JTAS-10XL30-DX). 3 HP, 240V. 90 depth and tilt inferred as class standard (not on jettools spec table).

machine_name: SawStop Industrial Cabinet Saw 3HP (ICS31230-TGI236, 36" T-Glide)
who_its_for: This is the heavy-duty SawStop for a busy shop or one that runs the saw hard all day. It suits pros, schools, and production settings that want the safety brake on a saw built like a tank. Home woodworkers who do not need that mass and duty cycle are usually better served by the PCS.
owner_praise: It is built like a tank, around 635 pounds, and runs quiet with almost no vibration | Factory fit and finish are excellent, with blade and miter slot dialed in tight | The dust-collection blade guard grabs nearly all the above-table dust | Three horsepower never runs short, and it tends to have fewer motor headaches than the 5HP
owner_limitations: The included extension table feels flimsier than some owners expect | A few felt a recent unit did not fully live up to the industrial billing | The 5HP versions have known start-capacitor wear, which nudges buyers to the 3HP | It is heavy, big, and pricey, and overkill for most home shops next to the PCS
upgrade_compatibility: Runs any 10-inch blade and an 8-inch dado stack up to 29/32 inch with the dado brake cartridge and insert. Works with SawStop overarm dust collection, router tables, and outfeed tables, converts to a 52 inch rip with longer rails, and rolls on the SawStop industrial mobile base.
compare_1_note: The SawStop PCS is the lighter, cheaper cabinet saw with the same brake.
compare_2_note: The Powermatic PM2000B is a premium 3 HP cabinet saw without the brake.
compare_3_note: The Jet XactaSaw Deluxe is a 3 HP cabinet saw at a lower price.
notes: New 7/6/2026. Price $5,154 starting VERIFIED on sawstop.com (3 HP, 36 in T-Glide). 3 HP, 240V. Assembled ~635 lbs.

machine_name: Grizzly G0858 8-Inch Parallelogram Jointer w/ Helical Cutterhead
who_its_for: Woodworkers who flatten hardwood often enough that tear-out has become a real problem. The 8-inch width and 76-inch bed handle long stock, and the helical head is the reason to buy this one. It needs a 230V circuit and it ships on a pallet at 487 pounds, so sort out power and delivery before it lands.
owner_praise: The helical cutterhead leaves a clean surface in figured maple and walnut where straight knives would tear|Parallelogram tables stay coplanar and are straightforward to dial back in|The 36 carbide inserts each rotate four times, so edge changes are rare and cheap|A mobile base is built in, so you can roll it out of the way without buying anything extra
owner_limitations: It runs on 230V single-phase, which rules it out for shops without a dedicated circuit|At 487 pounds with dock-only delivery, getting it into the shop is a genuine project|A few owners report the fence stops are worth checking against a square before you trust them
upgrade_compatibility: There isn't much to upgrade here since the helical head is already fitted. Replacement inserts are Grizzly H9893, about $40 for ten, and each insert turns four times before you replace it. Most owners just add a 4-inch dust hose and a decent push block.
notes: Jointer #1. Phase 1.4 category_specs join test. Price 2250 verified in-stock at grizzly.com 7/13/2026. 3HP 230V single-phase, dock delivery only. 4.7/5 from 224 reviews.

machine_name: Grizzly G0945 6-Inch Benchtop Jointer
who_its_for: A first jointer for someone who wants to square up small stock without giving a floor machine any space. It bolts to a bench, weighs 44 pounds, and does fine on short boards. If you already know you'll be flattening long hardwood, you'll outgrow it quickly.
owner_praise: Cheap enough to find out whether you actually need a jointer|Light enough to lift onto a bench and stow away when you're done|Cuts clean edges on short stock once the fence is squared|Two knives are easy to swap and cost about twenty dollars a set
owner_limitations: The extruded aluminum fence is light and flexes, and it's the complaint that comes up most|At 44 pounds it walks across the bench unless you clamp or bolt it down|A 30-inch bed is too short for long boards, so you'll need infeed and outfeed support
upgrade_compatibility: There isn't a real upgrade path here. Replacement knives are Grizzly T32900, about twenty dollars a pair. The honest upgrade is a bigger machine, and your money goes further moving up than modifying this one.
notes: SALE PRICE: $315 (list $325), ends 7/27/26 - recheck after. 4.7/5 from 77 reviews. Straight-knife, cast aluminum tables, 11,000 RPM.

machine_name: Grizzly G0946 6-Inch Benchtop Jointer with Spiral-Type Cutterhead
who_its_for: The same benchtop as the G0945 with an insert cutterhead instead of knives, for about four dollars more. If you're choosing between the two, this is the one to get. It runs quieter, leaves less tear-out, and you rotate a dull insert rather than resetting knives.
owner_praise: Arrives close to coplanar and needs very little setup, which is unusual at this price|Noticeably quieter with less tear-out than a straight-knife benchtop|Twelve two-sided inserts rotate to a fresh edge instead of needing sharpening|Small enough to store on a shelf between projects
